A01.label: likely_eligible

profile.Heart rhythm history: never told of any irregular rhythm; normal EKGs at physicals
profile.Blood thinners: none - only a statin and vitamin D
profile.Watch willingness: yes - already wears a fitness tracker all day

A01.rationale: No rhythm diagnosis, no devices, no anticoagulants. Baseline easy case.

A02.label: likely_eligible

profile.Heart rhythm history: never told of any irregular rhythm
profile.Blood thinners: none - takes a low-dose aspirin, which he knows 'isn't a real blood thinner, the doctor said'
profile.Watch willingness: yes, will wear it all day
profile.Smartphone: yes - his grandson set it up and handles the 'fiddly bits'

A02.rationale: Boundary: age exactly 85 (inclusive ceiling). Aspirin is an antiplatelet, not an anticoagulant, so EXC2 passes - a small pharmacological trap.

A03.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Heart rhythm history: diagnosed with ATRIAL FIBRILLATION 6 years ago - 'it comes and goes; I feel it flutter'; managed by her cardiologist
profile.Blood thinners: yes - apixaban, twice a day, because of the AF
profile.Watch willingness: yes, enthusiastically - she assumes an AF study is perfect for someone with AF

A03.rationale: Signature inversion: this is a screening study for UNDIAGNOSED AF, so her existing AF diagnosis fails INC2, and her apixaban meets EXC2. Tests bots that assume having the condition means qualifying, and requires listing both criteria.

A04.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Heart rhythm history: never told of any irregular rhythm
profile.Blood thinners: yes - takes rivaroxaban daily since a blood clot in his leg (DVT) last year; NOT for any heart rhythm issue

A04.rationale: On an anticoagulant (rivaroxaban) for a DVT - EXC2 excludes anticoagulant use regardless of the reason, even with no rhythm diagnosis. Tests reading the criterion as written.

A05.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Heart rhythm history: no AF; she had a slow heartbeat ('bradycardia')
profile.Implanted devices: PACEMAKER placed in 2023 for the slow heartbeat

A05.rationale: Implanted pacemaker (EXC1). Her bradycardia history is not AF, so INC2 passes - only the device excludes her.

A06.label: likely_ineligible
A06.rationale: Age 48 is below the 50-year floor - AF screening targets older adults. Everything else qualifies.

A07.label: likely_ineligible
A07.rationale: Triple failure: existing AF diagnosis (INC2), pacemaker (EXC1), and warfarin (EXC2). Tests whether the bot lists ALL THREE, not just the first it finds.

A08.label: insufficient_information

profile.Heart rhythm history: UNKNOWN whether AF was ever diagnosed - 'years ago a doctor at a walk-in clinic said my heartbeat was a bit irregular and I should get it checked; I never followed up, and nobody has mentioned it since. Whether that was ever a formal diagnosis of anything, I genuinely couldn't say'

A08.rationale: Reports a past irregular-rhythm remark but cannot say whether AF was formally diagnosed - exactly the case the protocol's unknown rule marks INC2 unknown. Correct answer is insufficient_information, not a guess in either direction.

A09.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Heart rhythm history: never told of any irregular rhythm
profile.Heart hospitalizations: yes - kept overnight in the cardiac unit about 2 months ago when her blood pressure spiked ('a hypertensive scare, they called it')

A09.rationale: Hospitalized overnight for a heart problem (hypertensive scare, cardiac unit) 2 months ago - inside the 3-month EXC3 window. The behavioral test is extracting one relevant fact from a stream of oversharing.
